obj/api/TradingSimulator.Strategies.Trad.title: Enum TradingDecisionEnum | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
obj/api/TradingSimulator.Strategies.Trad.summary: Enum TradingDecisionEnum Defines the possible trading decisions that can be made by a trading strategy. This enum is used throughout the trading simulator to represent the core actions a strategy can recommend: purchasing an asset, maintaining the current position, or selling an existing position. It serves as the fundamental decision type for strategy evaluation and trade execution logic. Namespace: TradingSimulator.Strategies Assembly: TradingSimulator.dll Syntax public enum TradingDecisionEnum Fields Name Description Buy Indicates a recommendation to purchase or enter a long position in the asset. This decision is typically generated when strategy signals suggest upward price movement or favorable market conditions for buying. Hold Indicates a recommendation to maintain the current position without making changes. This decision is used when strategy signals do not strongly suggest buying or selling, or when the market conditions are neutral or uncertain. Sell Indicates a recommendation to sell or exit a long position in the asset. This decision is typically generated when strategy signals suggest downward price movement or unfavorable market conditions for holding the current position.

obj/api/TradingSimulator.TradingSimulato.title: Class TradingSimulatorService | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
obj/api/TradingSimulator.TradingSimulato.summary: Class TradingSimulatorService Core service for orchestrating trading strategy simulations and backtesting operations. This service manages the complete lifecycle of running trading strategies against historical market snapshots, including data loading, strategy execution, performance analysis, and result reporting. It integrates with various components like DataLoader, MarketProcessor, and StrategyResolver to provide comprehensive simulation capabilities for evaluating trading strategies. Features include configurable cache directory and timeout values, input validation with warnings for invalid parameters, and async operations for high-throughput scenarios. obj/api/TradingSimulator.html.title: Namespace TradingSimulator | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
obj/api/TradingSimulator.html.summary: Namespace TradingSimulator Classes CachedMarketData Contains cached market data for a specific market, including profit/loss, position information, and various lists of price points representing different market events and states. This class serves as the primary data structure for storing processed market simulation results that can be serialized to JSON for persistence and later visualization in the GUI. DataLoader MarketProcessor Processes individual markets in the trading simulator by running trading strategies, analyzing event logs, and generating visualization data points for market analysis. This class orchestrates the complete market processing pipeline from simulation to data persistence. PricePoint Represents a point in time with a price value and optional memo for market data visualization. This record is used throughout the trading simulator to store timestamped price information with associated metadata for charting and analysis purposes. StrategyResolver Central resolver for trading strategy families in the trading simulator. This class acts as a bridge between strategy family enums and their concrete implementations, providing a unified interface for accessing training mappings and parameter sets for different trading strategies. It encapsulates the logic for resolving strategy families to their corresponding configurations and mappings. TradePoint Represents a trade point with timestamp, price, and trading decision. This record encapsulates the essential data for a trading action in the simulator. TradingContext TradingSimulatorService Core service for orchestrating trading strategy simulations and backtesting operations. This service manages the complete lifecycle of running trading strategies against historical market snapshots, including data loading, strategy execution, performance analysis, and result reporting. It integrates with various components like DataLoader, MarketProcessor, and StrategyResolver to provide comprehensive simulation capabilities for evaluating trading strategies. Features include configurable cache directory and timeout values, input validation with warnings for invalid parameters, and async operations for high-throughput scenarios. Enums TradingSimulatorService.StrategyFamily Delegates TradingStrategyFunc<T>

obj/api/TradingStrategies.ActionDecision.title: Class ActionDecision | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
obj/api/TradingStrategies.ActionDecision.summary: Class ActionDecision Represents the output of a trading strategy's decision-making process in the Kalshi trading bot system. This class encapsulates the recommended trading action, including the action type, associated parameters for order execution, and explanatory information. It serves as the communication mechanism between trading strategies and the simulation or execution engine, providing all necessary details for processing the recommended trade action. The Type property determines the primary action (Buy, Sell, Exit, None, etc.), while Price and Quantity specify order parameters for limit orders. Expiration allows for time-limited orders, and Memo provides context for the decision that can be used for logging, analysis, or debugging. obj/api/TradingStrategies.Classification.title: Class SnapshotPeriodHelper | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
obj/api/TradingStrategies.Classification.summary: Class SnapshotPeriodHelper Provides functionality for processing and grouping market snapshots into valid analysis periods. This class implements the core logic for identifying continuous market periods based on time gaps and price stability, ensuring that only stable market conditions are used for trading strategy evaluation and backtesting. It handles the serialization of snapshot groups to disk and their subsequent loading for analysis. Inheritance object SnapshotPeriodHelper Implements ISnapshotPeriodHelper Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: TradingStrategies.Classification Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public class SnapshotPeriodHelper : ISnapshotPeriodHelper Remarks The class uses configurable thresholds for time gaps (smallGapMinutes and maxActiveGapHours) to determine period boundaries. Snapshots are grouped when they are temporally close and price changes are minimal, allowing for meaningful analysis of market behavior during stable periods. Methods | Edit this page View Source LoadSnapshotGroup(string) Loads a snapshot group from a JSON file and reconstructs the original snapshot DTOs. This method reverses the flattening process performed during group creation, converting the stored flat data back into structured SnapshotDTO objects with proper JSON formatting. Declaration public List<SnapshotDTO> LoadSnapshotGroup(string filePath) Parameters Type Name Description string filePath The full path to the JSON file containing the flattened snapshot group data. Returns Type Description List<SnapshotDTO> A list of reconstructed SnapshotDTO objects, ordered by snapshot date. Remarks The loading process: Reads the JSON file containing flattened snapshot data Deserializes into a list of flattened dictionaries Reconstructs each snapshot's RawJSON by mapping flattened fields back to MarketSnapshot structure Orders snapshots by timestamp for consistency Enhances snapshots with market type information for downstream processing Error handling ensures that file reading or parsing failures don't crash the application, with errors logged for debugging while returning any successfully processed snapshots. | Edit this page View Source SplitIntoValidGroups(List<SnapshotDTO>, string) Splits a sequence of market snapshots into valid analysis periods based on time continuity and price stability criteria. Snapshots are grouped together when they represent a continuous market period without significant price changes or excessive time gaps. Declaration public List<SnapshotGroupDTO> SplitIntoValidGroups(List<SnapshotDTO> snapshots, string snapshotDirectory) Parameters Type Name Description List<SnapshotDTO> snapshots The ordered list of market snapshots to process. Must be sorted by timestamp. string snapshotDirectory The directory path where snapshot group files will be saved. Returns Type Description List<SnapshotGroupDTO> A list of snapshot groups, each representing a valid analysis period with aggregated data. Remarks The method processes snapshots sequentially, starting a new group when: Time gaps exceed the maximum active gap threshold, or Price changes exceed the threshold (unless within the small gap tolerance) Each group is saved as a JSON file and returned as a SnapshotGroupDTO containing start/end times, price ranges, liquidity metrics, and file path information. Implements ISnapshotPeriodHelper

obj/api/TradingStrategies.Configuration..summary: Class SnapshotConfig Configuration class containing snapshot-related parameters used throughout the trading bot system. These settings control snapshot timing tolerances, schema versioning, and data persistence behavior for market data snapshots used in trading strategy evaluation and backtesting. Used by TradingSnapshotService for schema validation and snapshot upgrading during loading. Used to determine acceptable deviations from expected snapshot intervals before triggering warnings or discarding snapshots. Higher values provide more flexibility for irregular market data arrival but may mask timing issues. Typically set to match expected decision frequency intervals (e.g., 30-60 seconds). Values are injected via dependency injection and consumed by TradingStrategy, TradingSnapshotService, and related components for consistent timing behavior. obj/api/TradingStrategies.Extensions.Mar.title: Class MarketSnapshotExtensions | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
obj/api/TradingStrategies.Extensions.Mar.summary: Class MarketSnapshotExtensions Provides extension methods for MarketSnapshot to compute and update order book metrics from simulated order book data. This class serves as a bridge between the raw SimulatedOrderbook data structure and the calculated metrics stored in MarketSnapshot, enabling efficient computation of spreads, depths, volumes, and other trading indicators. All calculations are performed in a stateless manner, ensuring thread safety and reusability across different market simulation scenarios. Inheritance object MarketSnapshotExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: TradingStrategies.Extensions Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public static class MarketSnapshotExtensions Methods | Edit this page View Source UpdateOrderbookMetricsFromSimulated(MarketSnapshot, SimulatedOrderbook) Updates all order book-related metrics in the MarketSnapshot based on the current state of the SimulatedOrderbook. This method recalculates spreads, depths, volumes, ranges, imbalances, and center of mass values to reflect the latest order book state. It is typically called after order book modifications during trading simulations to ensure all derived metrics remain consistent with the underlying order book data. Declaration public static void UpdateOrderbookMetricsFromSimulated(this MarketSnapshot snapshot, SimulatedOrderbook book) Parameters Type Name Description MarketSnapshot snapshot The MarketSnapshot instance whose metrics will be updated. SimulatedOrderbook book The SimulatedOrderbook containing the current order book state to analyze. Remarks This method performs comprehensive order book analysis including: Best bid/ask price identification Spread calculations between bid and ask prices Depth analysis at best prices and within tolerance ranges Volume calculations in both contract count and dollar value Price range analysis for bid orders Imbalance calculations between yes/no sides Center of mass calculations for order book distribution All calculations use the snapshot's TolerancePercentage for range-based metrics and assume prices are in cents (1-99 range) as per Kalshi market conventions.

obj/api/TradingStrategies.Extensions.Pse.title: Class PseudoCandlestickExtensions | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
obj/api/TradingStrategies.Extensions.Pse.summary: Class PseudoCandlestickExtensions Provides extension methods for converting PseudoCandlestick collections to standard candlestick formats. This class serves as a bridge between simplified mid-price candlestick data and traditional OHLC candlestick data, enabling pattern detection and technical analysis algorithms to work with market data. Inheritance object PseudoCandlestickExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: TradingStrategies.Extensions Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public static class PseudoCandlestickExtensions Methods | Edit this page View Source ToCandleMids(IList<PseudoCandlestick>, string) Converts a sequence of PseudoCandlesticks to an array of CandleMids with OHLC data. Each resulting candlestick represents the price movement between consecutive PseudoCandlesticks, using the previous candlestick's mid-close as the open price and the current candlestick's mid-close as the close price, with high/low from the current candlestick. Declaration public static CandleMids[] ToCandleMids(this IList<PseudoCandlestick> candles, string marketTicker) Parameters Type Name Description IList<PseudoCandlestick> candles The sequence of PseudoCandlesticks to convert. Must contain at least 2 elements. string marketTicker The market ticker identifier to assign to each resulting CandleMids. Returns Type Description CandleMids[] An array of CandleMids representing the price movements between consecutive input candlesticks. Remarks This method creates n-1 candlesticks from n input PseudoCandlesticks, where each output candlestick represents the transition from one time period to the next. The volume is cast from decimal to double to match the CandleMids data type expectations. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentNullException Thrown when candles is null. ArgumentException Thrown when candles contains fewer than 2 elements.

obj/api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.Sig.title: Enum Signal | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
obj/api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.Sig.summary: Enum Signal Defines the various trading signals used by the strategy system to identify market conditions and technical indicators. These signals represent different market states, technical analysis patterns, and external factors that can influence trading decisions. Each signal corresponds to a specific condition that can be evaluated against market data to determine appropriate trading actions. Namespace: TradingStrategies.Strategies Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public enum Signal Fields Name Description AbsPositionAboveMax Absolute position size is above the maximum allowed threshold. ActiveYes Yes side showing active trading interest. AtrMediumHigh Average True Range (ATR) indicates medium to high volatility. BearishMomentum Bearish momentum indicators are present. BearishVolume Bearish volume patterns detected. BidImbalance Bid side shows imbalance compared to ask side. BreakoutDown Price breaking out to the downside. BreakoutUp Price breaking out to the upside. BullishMomentum Bullish momentum indicators are present. BullishVolume Bullish volume patterns detected. BuyFlow Strong buying flow detected in the market. BuyPressure Strong buying pressure detected. CryptoNews Cryptocurrency-related news impacting markets. DepthAtBestYesAskLow Depth at the best Yes ask is low, indicating thin liquidity on the sell side. DepthAtBestYesBidLow Depth at the best Yes bid is low, indicating thin liquidity on the buy side. ElectionNearing Election timing is nearing, potentially affecting market volatility. ExtremeWeather Extreme weather conditions affecting market sentiment. FadeHigh Price fading from recent highs. FadeLow Price fading from recent lows. HighVolBuy High volume buying activity detected. HighVolSell High volume selling activity detected. LargeBuyTrades Large buy trades executed. LiveEvent Live event occurring that may impact market outcomes. MacdAboveSignal MACD line is above the signal line, indicating bullish momentum. MacdBelowSignal MACD line is below the signal line, indicating bearish momentum. MacroEvent Macroeconomic event influencing market conditions. MidAboveBollingerUpper Mid price is above the upper Bollinger Band, suggesting potential reversal or overextension. MidAboveEmaMedium Mid price is above the medium-term EMA. MidAboveEmaPlusAtr Mid price is above the EMA plus ATR band, indicating potential overbought conditions. MidAboveVwap Mid price is above the Volume Weighted Average Price (VWAP). MidBelowBollingerLower Mid price is below the lower Bollinger Band, suggesting potential reversal or overextension. MidBelowEmaMedium Mid price is below the medium-term EMA. MidBelowEmaMinusAtr Mid price is below the EMA minus ATR band, indicating potential oversold conditions. MidBelowVwap Mid price is below the Volume Weighted Average Price (VWAP). NearResistance Price is near a resistance level, potentially indicating a reversal opportunity. NearSupport Price is near a support level, potentially indicating a bounce opportunity. NegativeHistogram MACD histogram shows negative divergence. NegativeOBV On-Balance Volume (OBV) shows negative divergence. ObvMedium On-Balance Volume (OBV) indicates medium-term accumulation/distribution. Overbought Market indicators suggest overbought conditions. Oversold Market indicators suggest oversold conditions. PositiveHistogram MACD histogram shows positive divergence. PositiveOBV On-Balance Volume (OBV) shows positive divergence. RateCutExpectation Market expectation of interest rate cuts. RsiShort RSI indicates short-term overbought conditions. SeasonalPattern Seasonal pattern influencing market behavior. SellFlow Strong selling flow detected in the market. SellPressure Strong selling pressure detected. StochasticK Stochastic K oscillator reading. TeamFavorite Team or competitor favoritism influencing market sentiment. TradeVolumePerMinuteNoHigh Trade volume per minute for No contracts is high. TradeVolumePerMinuteYesHigh Trade volume per minute for Yes contracts is high. UndervaluedCandidate Asset appears to be undervalued relative to fundamentals. VelocityPerMinuteTopNoBid Velocity per minute at the top No bid price level. VelocityPerMinuteTopYesBid Velocity per minute at the top Yes bid price level. WideSpreadBuy Wide spread conditions favoring buy orders. WideSpreadSell Wide spread conditions favoring sell orders.

obj/api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.Str.title: Class Strategy | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
obj/api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.Str.summary: Class Strategy Represents a composite trading strategy that combines multiple individual strategies (Strats). This class aggregates the decisions from multiple strategies based on their weights, implementing a voting system where the strategy with the highest combined weight for a particular action wins. The composite approach allows for more robust trading decisions by considering multiple perspectives. obj/api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.htm.title: Namespace TradingStrategies.Strategies | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
obj/api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.htm.summary: Namespace TradingStrategies.Strategies Classes Scenario Represents a trading scenario that organizes strategies based on different market conditions. This class serves as a container for mapping market types to their corresponding trading strategies, allowing the system to select appropriate strategies based on current market conditions during simulation or live trading. SignalCondition Defines a condition based on a trading signal that triggers a specific trading action. This class combines a signal (technical indicator or market condition), a comparison operator, a threshold value, and the resulting action type to create rule-based trading logic. Signal conditions are used by strategies to evaluate market states and determine appropriate responses. Strat Abstract base class for individual trading strategy implementations. Concrete strategy classes inherit from this class to provide specific trading logic that evaluates market conditions and returns trading decisions. Each strategy has a weight that influences its importance when combined with other strategies in a composite approach. Strategy Represents a composite trading strategy that combines multiple individual strategies (Strats). This class aggregates the decisions from multiple strategies based on their weights, implementing a voting system where the strategy with the highest combined weight for a particular action wins. The composite approach allows for more robust trading decisions by considering multiple perspectives. Enums Signal Defines the various trading signals used by the strategy system to identify market conditions and technical indicators. These signals represent different market states, technical analysis patterns, and external factors that can influence trading decisions. Each signal corresponds to a specific condition that can be evaluated against market data to determine appropriate trading actions.

obj/api/TradingStrategies.Trading.Helper.title: Class MarketTypeHelper | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
obj/api/TradingStrategies.Trading.Helper.summary: Class MarketTypeHelper Provides functionality to classify market types based on various trading conditions extracted from market snapshots. This helper uses a rule-based system with priority ordering to determine the most appropriate market type for a given set of market conditions, enabling trading strategies to adapt their behavior accordingly. It extracts six key condition types from the snapshot (price movement, liquidity, closing time, activity level, uncertainty signal, and market category) and uses them to find the best matching market type rule based on priority and condition specificity. The method is designed to be called during market analysis, simulation, and strategy selection phases.

obj/api/TradingStrategies.Trading.Overse.title: Class EquityCalculator | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
obj/api/TradingStrategies.Trading.Overse.summary: Class EquityCalculator Provides methods for calculating the total equity value of a trading simulation path. This class is responsible for determining the current market value of positions held in a simulated trading environment, taking into account both cash holdings and the unrealized value of open positions based on current order book data. Inheritance object EquityCalculator Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: TradingStrategies.Trading.Overseer Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public class EquityCalculator Remarks The EquityCalculator is a critical component in the trading simulation pipeline, used by the StrategySimulation engine to evaluate the performance of trading strategies. It handles different market conditions (natural vs. non-natural markets) and provides accurate equity calculations that reflect real-world trading mechanics. Key responsibilities: Calculate total equity as cash plus position value Handle natural markets where one side has no liquidity Use mid-prices for non-natural markets to estimate fair value Support both long and short positions This class is stateless and thread-safe, making it suitable for concurrent simulation runs. Methods | Edit this page View Source GetEquity(SimulationPath, MarketSnapshot) Calculates the total equity value for a given simulation path at the current market snapshot. Declaration public double GetEquity(SimulationPath path, MarketSnapshot lastSnapshot) Parameters Type Name Description SimulationPath path The simulation path containing cash, position, and order book state. MarketSnapshot lastSnapshot The most recent market snapshot containing current market data (used for context but not directly for calculation). Returns Type Description double The total equity value in dollars, including cash and the current market value of all positions. Remarks This method implements the core equity calculation logic used throughout the trading simulation system. The calculation follows these steps: Start with the cash balance from the simulation path If no simulated order book exists, return cash only Extract best bid/ask prices from the simulated order book Determine if the market is "natural" (one side has no bids) For natural markets: Long positions (positive): Value at 1.0 if no bids on the short side, 0.0 otherwise Short positions (negative): Value at 1.0 if no bids on the long side, 0.0 otherwise For non-natural markets: Use mid-prices (average of best bid and ask) to value positions Long positions valued at mid-price of the "Yes" side Short positions valued at mid-price of the "No" side The method ensures accurate valuation that reflects the current state of the simulated market, providing a realistic assessment of portfolio value for strategy evaluation and backtesting purposes. Example usage: var calculator = new EquityCalculator(); double totalEquity = calculator.GetEquity(simulationPath, marketSnapshot); Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentNullException Thrown if path is null. InvalidOperationException Thrown if the simulated order book data is corrupted.

obj/api/TradingStrategies.Trading.Overse.title: Class MarketTypeService | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
obj/api/TradingStrategies.Trading.Overse.summary: Class MarketTypeService Service responsible for determining and caching market types for trading snapshots. This class acts as a facade over the MarketTypeHelper, providing caching functionality to avoid redundant market type calculations for the same market snapshot. Inheritance object MarketTypeService Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: TradingStrategies.Trading.Overseer Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public class MarketTypeService Remarks The MarketTypeService is used in the trading simulation pipeline to classify market conditions based on various indicators (price movement, liquidity, activity, etc.). It leverages a helper class to perform the actual classification logic and maintains an in-memory cache to optimize performance during simulation runs where the same snapshot may be processed multiple times. Key responsibilities: Assign market types to MarketSnapshot instances Cache market type results to reduce computational overhead Convert string representations back to MarketType enums This service is instantiated once per simulation engine and reused throughout the simulation process. Constructors | Edit this page View Source MarketTypeService() Initializes a new instance of the MarketTypeService class. Declaration public MarketTypeService() Remarks Creates the MarketTypeHelper instance and initializes the cache dictionary. This constructor sets up the service for immediate use in market type classification. Methods | Edit this page View Source AssignMarketTypeToSnapshot(MarketSnapshot) Assigns the appropriate market type to the provided market snapshot. Declaration public void AssignMarketTypeToSnapshot(MarketSnapshot snapshot) Parameters Type Name Description MarketSnapshot snapshot The market snapshot to classify and update with market type information. Remarks This method first checks the cache for an existing classification result. If not found, it delegates to the MarketTypeHelper to determine the market type based on the snapshot's trading conditions. The result is then cached and assigned to the snapshot's MarketType property. If an exception occurs during classification (e.g., due to missing or invalid data), the market type is set to "Unknown" as a fallback to ensure simulation continuity. The method modifies the input snapshot in-place, setting its MarketType property to the string representation of the determined MarketType enum value. | Edit this page View Source ConvertStringToMarketType(string) Converts a string representation of a market type to its corresponding MarketType enum value. Declaration public StrategyEnums.MarketType ConvertStringToMarketType(string marketType) Parameters Type Name Description string marketType The string representation of the market type to convert. Returns Type Description StrategyEnums.MarketType The MarketType enum value corresponding to the input string, or MarketType.Undefined if parsing fails. Remarks This method performs case-insensitive parsing of the market type string using Enum.TryParse. It serves as a utility for converting market type strings (e.g., from serialized data or user input) back to strongly-typed enum values for use in conditional logic and strategy selection. If the string cannot be parsed to a valid MarketType value, MarketType.Undefined is returned as a safe default to prevent exceptions in calling code. Common use cases include: Deserializing market type data from persistent storage Processing market type strings from external APIs Converting user-provided market type filters

obj/api/TradingStrategies.Trading.Overse.title: Class PatternDetectionService | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
obj/api/TradingStrategies.Trading.Overse.summary: Class PatternDetectionService Service responsible for detecting candlestick patterns from market snapshots in the Kalshi trading bot system. This service acts as a bridge between market data and pattern recognition algorithms, enabling trading strategies to incorporate technical analysis based on historical price movements. The service processes market snapshots containing recent candlestick data, converts them to a format suitable for pattern analysis, and applies comprehensive pattern detection algorithms to identify various technical analysis formations including single-candle, multi-candle, and complex patterns. Key responsibilities: Convert market snapshot candlestick data to pattern analysis format Execute pattern detection algorithms across multiple timeframes and pattern types Filter and return the most relevant patterns for trading decision making Handle edge cases and provide graceful fallbacks for data processing errors This service is used by the SimulationEngine during backtesting and strategy evaluation to enrich market analysis with technical pattern recognition capabilities. Inheritance object PatternDetectionService Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: TradingStrategies.Trading.Overseer Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public class PatternDetectionService Remarks The pattern detection process involves several steps: Validation of input data (candlestick availability) Conversion of PseudoCandlesticks to CandleMids format for analysis Application of pattern recognition algorithms with trend analysis Pattern filtering to remove redundancies and prioritize significant formations Return of filtered patterns for strategy consumption Error handling ensures that pattern detection failures don't interrupt the overall trading simulation, with appropriate logging for debugging and monitoring purposes. Methods | Edit this page View Source DetectPatterns(MarketSnapshot) Detects candlestick patterns from the provided market snapshot. Analyzes recent candlestick data to identify various technical analysis patterns that can inform trading strategy decisions. Declaration public List<PatternDefinition> DetectPatterns(MarketSnapshot snapshot) Parameters Type Name Description MarketSnapshot snapshot The market snapshot containing recent candlestick data and market information. Returns Type Description List<PatternDefinition> A list of detected pattern definitions representing significant technical formations. Remarks The detection process involves: Converting PseudoCandlesticks to CandleMids format for pattern analysis Applying comprehensive pattern recognition algorithms across multiple pattern types Using a 10-period lookback window for trend context and pattern validation Filtering patterns to return only the most recent and relevant formations If no candlestick data is available or pattern detection fails, an empty list is returned to ensure graceful degradation in the trading simulation pipeline. The method handles various edge cases: Null or empty candlestick collections Pattern detection algorithm failures Data conversion errors All exceptions are caught and logged to prevent interruption of the simulation process, with appropriate fallback behavior ensuring system stability. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentNullException Thrown when snapshot is null (handled internally). Exception Any pattern detection errors are caught and logged internally.

obj/api/TradingStrategies.Trading.Overse.title: Class ReportGenerator | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
obj/api/TradingStrategies.Trading.Overse.summary: Class ReportGenerator Provides static methods for generating comprehensive performance reports from trading simulation event logs. This class processes collections of SimulationEventLog objects to produce detailed CSV-formatted reports that analyze trading performance, market statistics, strategy effectiveness, and simulation outcomes. It serves as the core reporting engine for the trading overseer system, enabling detailed analysis of backtesting results and strategy evaluation through structured data exports. Inheritance object ReportGenerator Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: TradingStrategies.Trading.Overseer Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public class ReportGenerator Methods | Edit this page View Source GenerateDetailedPerformanceReport(string?, List<SimulationEventLog>, double, Dictionary<string, PathInfo>, bool, string) Generates a comprehensive detailed performance report from trading simulation event logs. This method processes a collection of SimulationEventLog entries to create a multi-section CSV report containing summary statistics, market distribution analysis, order book summaries, full event logs, summarized event timelines, and path definitions. The report provides detailed insights into trading performance, market conditions, and strategy effectiveness. Declaration public string GenerateDetailedPerformanceReport(string? marketId, List<ReportGenerator.SimulationEventLog> events, double initialCash, Dictionary<string, ReportGenerator.PathInfo> paths, bool writeToFile, string outputDir = "C:\\Users\\Peter\\Documents\\GitHub\\TestingOutput") Parameters Type Name Description string marketId The unique identifier of the market being analyzed. List<ReportGenerator.SimulationEventLog> events The collection of event logs from the trading simulation. double initialCash The starting cash balance for the simulation. Dictionary<string, ReportGenerator.PathInfo> paths Dictionary mapping path identifiers to their strategy information. bool writeToFile Whether to write the report to a file in addition to returning it as a string. string outputDir The directory path where the report file should be written. Returns Type Description string A string containing the complete CSV-formatted performance report. | Edit this page View Source GenerateFinalPerformanceReport(string?, string, Dictionary<string, int>, double, double, string, bool, string) Generates a final performance report for a completed trading simulation path. This method creates a simple CSV report summarizing the key outcomes of a trading path, including profit/loss, final equity, trade counts, and market state transitions. Declaration public string GenerateFinalPerformanceReport(string? marketId, string pathTaken, Dictionary<string, int> snapshotsPerType, double pnl, double finalEquity, string notes, bool writeToFile, string outputDir = "C:\\Users\\Peter\\Documents\\GitHub\\TestingOutput") Parameters Type Name Description string marketId The unique identifier of the market. string pathTaken The sequence of market types or path taken. Dictionary<string, int> snapshotsPerType Dictionary mapping market types to snapshot counts. double pnl The profit and loss for the trading path. double finalEquity The final equity value. string notes Additional notes or observations about the performance. bool writeToFile Whether to write the report to a file. string outputDir The directory path for file output. Returns Type Description string A string containing the CSV-formatted final performance report. | Edit this page View Source GenerateRollupReport(List<PathPerformance>, bool, string) Generates a comprehensive rollup report aggregating performance data across multiple trading paths. This method processes a collection of PathPerformance objects to create a CSV report that summarizes performance metrics across different markets and paths, including aggregate statistics for overall analysis. The report is sorted by final equity within each market group for easy comparison. Declaration public string GenerateRollupReport(List<ReportGenerator.PathPerformance> allPerformances, bool writeToFile, string outputDir = "C:\\Users\\Peter\\Documents\\GitHub\\TestingOutput") Parameters Type Name Description List<ReportGenerator.PathPerformance> allPerformances The collection of path performance data to aggregate. bool writeToFile Whether to write the report to a file. string outputDir The directory path for file output. obj/api/TradingStrategies.Trading.Overse.title: Class SimulationEngine | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
obj/api/TradingStrategies.Trading.Overse.summary: Class SimulationEngine Core engine for executing trading strategy simulations against historical market data. This class orchestrates the complete simulation lifecycle, processing market snapshots sequentially, applying trading strategies, simulating order execution and fills, managing position and cash flow, and generating detailed event logs for backtesting and performance analysis. Inheritance object SimulationEngine Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: TradingStrategies.Trading.Overseer Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public class SimulationEngine Remarks The SimulationEngine serves as the central component in the trading simulation pipeline, bridging raw market data with strategy logic to produce realistic trading outcomes. It handles complex scenarios including multi-strategy execution, order book simulation, resting order management, and risk controls. Constructors | Edit this page View Source SimulationEngine() Initializes a new instance of the SimulationEngine with required services. Declaration public SimulationEngine() Remarks Creates instances of MarketTypeService and PatternDetectionService for market classification and pattern recognition during simulation. Methods | Edit this page View Source RunSimulation(Scenario, List<MarketSnapshot>, bool) Executes a complete trading simulation against a sequence of market snapshots. Declaration public List<SimulationPath> RunSimulation(Scenario scenario, List<MarketSnapshot> snapshots, bool isSingleStrategy) Parameters Type Name Description Scenario scenario The trading scenario containing strategies organized by market conditions. List<MarketSnapshot> snapshots The sequence of market snapshots to simulate against, in chronological order. bool isSingleStrategy Whether to run in single-strategy mode (modifies paths in-place) or multi-strategy mode (creates new path branches). Returns Type Description List<SimulationPath> A list of completed simulation paths, each representing a possible outcome of the trading scenario. Remarks This method orchestrates the entire simulation process: Initializes simulation paths with starting conditions Processes each snapshot sequentially, applying market changes and strategy decisions Manages order book state, position tracking, and cash flow Generates detailed event logs for analysis Handles strategy branching in multi-strategy mode The simulation supports realistic trading mechanics including: Order book depth changes and fill simulation Resting order management and expiration Risk controls and position limits Multiple concurrent strategy paths

obj/api/TradingStrategies.Trading.Overse.title: Class SimulationPath | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
obj/api/TradingStrategies.Trading.Overse.summary: Class SimulationPath Represents the complete state of a single trading simulation path in the Kalshi trading bot system. This class encapsulates all the essential data needed to track the evolution of a trading strategy simulation over time, including position, cash balance, risk metrics, and order book state. Inheritance object SimulationPath Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: TradingStrategies.Trading.Overseer Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public class SimulationPath Remarks SimulationPath serves as the core data container for the trading simulation engine, maintaining the state that evolves as strategies are executed against historical market data. It provides a snapshot of the trading account at any point during the simulation, enabling accurate performance evaluation and risk assessment. Key responsibilities include: - Tracking current position (positive for long, negative for short) - Maintaining cash balance and cumulative trading costs - Storing strategy configurations by market conditions - Managing simulated order book and resting orders - Recording simulation events for analysis - Calculating derived metrics like average entry price This class is used extensively by the SimulationEngine for branching simulations, by EquityCalculator for portfolio valuation, and by TradingOverseer for performance reporting. The immutable StrategiesByMarketConditions ensures consistent strategy application throughout the simulation lifecycle. Constructors | Edit this page View Source SimulationPath(Dictionary<MarketType, HashSet<Strategy>>, int, double) Initializes a new instance of the SimulationPath class. Declaration public SimulationPath(Dictionary<StrategyEnums.MarketType, HashSet<Strategy>> strategiesByMarketConditions, int position, double cash) Parameters Type Name Description Dictionary<StrategyEnums.MarketType, HashSet<Strategy>> strategiesByMarketConditions The mapping of market conditions to available strategies that will be used throughout the simulation. int position The initial position size (positive for long, negative for short). double cash The initial cash balance in dollars. Remarks Creates a new simulation path with the specified strategy configuration and starting conditions. The strategy mapping is stored as read-only to ensure consistency throughout the simulation. Other properties are initialized to default values and will be updated as the simulation progresses. Properties | Edit this page View Source AverageCost Gets the average entry price for the current position, regardless of direction. Declaration public double AverageCost { get; } Property Value Type Description double The average price paid/received per contract for the current position. Returns 0.0 if no position is held. Remarks This calculated property provides the average cost basis for the current position, which is essential for performance analysis and risk management. The calculation differs based on position direction: For long positions (positive): Returns TotalPaid divided by position size, representing the average price paid to acquire the contracts. For short positions (negative): Returns TotalReceived divided by absolute position size, representing the average price received when selling the contracts (entry price for shorts). This metric is used by trading strategies and performance reports to assess entry efficiency and calculate unrealized gains/losses relative to entry price. | Edit this page View Source Cash Gets or sets the current cash balance available for trading. Declaration public double Cash { get; set; } Property Value Type Description double The cash balance in dollars, updated after each trade execution. Remarks Cash is reduced when buying positions and increased when selling. Trading fees are also deducted from this balance. This value is critical for determining buying power and overall portfolio value. | Edit this page View Source CurrentRisk Gets or sets the current risk level of the position. Declaration public double CurrentRisk { get; set; } Property Value Type Description double The risk metric, defaulting to 0.0. Higher values indicate greater exposure. Remarks This property tracks the risk associated with the current position. It may be calculated based on position size, volatility, or other risk factors. Used by risk management components to enforce position limits. | Edit this page View Source Events Gets or sets the list of events that occurred during the simulation. Declaration public List<ReportGenerator.SimulationEventLog> Events { get; set; } Property Value Type Description List<ReportGenerator.SimulationEventLog> A list of ReportGenerator.SimulationEventLog entries recording simulation events. Remarks Events capture significant occurrences during simulation such as trades, strategy decisions, market condition changes, and errors. This log is used for performance analysis, debugging, and generating detailed simulation reports. | Edit this page View Source Position Gets or sets the current position size in the simulated market. Declaration public int Position { get; set; } Property Value Type Description int Positive values indicate long positions, negative values indicate short positions. Zero indicates no position. The magnitude represents the number of contracts held. Remarks This value is updated by the simulation engine as trades are executed. It directly affects equity calculations and risk assessments throughout the simulation. | Edit this page View Source SimulatedBook Gets or sets the simulated order book representing current market liquidity. Declaration public SimulatedOrderbook? SimulatedBook { get; set; } Property Value Type Description SimulatedOrderbook A SimulatedOrderbook instance containing bid/ask levels, or null if no order book has been initialized for this simulation path. Remarks The simulated order book tracks available liquidity at different price levels. It evolves during simulation as orders are placed, filled, and cancelled. Used by the simulation engine for realistic trade execution and by equity calculator for position valuation. | Edit this page View Source SimulatedRestingOrders Gets or sets the list of resting orders currently active in the simulation. Declaration public List<(string action, string side, string type, int count, int price, DateTime? expiration)> SimulatedRestingOrders { get; set; } Property Value Type Description List<(string action, string side, string type, int count, int price, DateTime? expiration)> A list of tuples representing resting limit orders, where each tuple contains: action: "buy" or "sell" side: "yes" or "no" type: order type (typically "limit") count: number of contracts price: limit price (1-99) expiration: optional expiration timestamp Remarks Resting orders represent unfilled limit orders that remain in the order book. They can be filled by market movements or cancelled by strategy decisions. This list is maintained in parallel with the simulated order book for consistency. | Edit this page View Source StrategiesByMarketConditions Gets the mapping of market conditions to available trading strategies. This dictionary determines which strategies are eligible for execution based on current market type. Declaration public Dictionary<StrategyEnums.MarketType, HashSet<Strategy>> StrategiesByMarketConditions { get; } Property Value Type Description Dictionary<StrategyEnums.MarketType, HashSet<Strategy>> A read-only dictionary where keys are StrategyEnums.MarketType values and values are sets of Strategy objects that can be applied in those market conditions. Remarks This mapping is established at simulation initialization and remains constant throughout the simulation. It enables the simulation engine to select appropriate strategies based on detected market conditions, supporting adaptive trading behavior. | Edit this page View Source TotalPaid Gets or sets the cumulative amount paid for establishing long positions. Declaration public double TotalPaid { get; set; } Property Value Type Description double The total dollars paid for buying contracts in long positions. Remarks This accumulator tracks all cash outflows for position entries. Combined with TotalReceived, it enables calculation of net trading costs and average entry prices for performance analysis. | Edit this page View Source TotalReceived Gets or sets the cumulative amount received from establishing short positions. Declaration public double TotalReceived { get; set; } Property Value Type Description double The total dollars received from selling contracts in short positions. Remarks This accumulator tracks all cash inflows from position entries. For short positions, this represents the entry price received when selling. Used alongside TotalPaid for comprehensive cost tracking.

obj/api/TradingStrategies.Trading.Overse.title: Class StrategySimulation | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
obj/api/TradingStrategies.Trading.Overse.summary: Class StrategySimulation Simulates trading strategies against historical market snapshots, managing position, cash, and order book state. This class processes market data sequentially, applies trading decisions from strategies, and tracks performance metrics. It maintains a simulated order book and resting orders to accurately model market interactions during backtesting. As a developer, this class is the core execution engine for strategy evaluation, handling the complex interplay between market data, strategy decisions, order book dynamics, and position management. Inheritance object StrategySimulation Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: TradingStrategies.Trading.Overseer Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public class StrategySimulation Remarks The simulation handles various action types including market orders, limit orders, and position exits. It applies realistic trading fees and ensures FIFO order matching for accurate simulation results. Key simulation mechanics include: Delta-based order book updates for efficiency Realistic fee calculation (0.07% taker fees) Position and cash tracking with proper accounting Resting order management with expiration handling Combo actions (take then rest) for advanced strategies Order book depth reduction and fill simulation Constructors | Edit this page View Source StrategySimulation(Strategy, double) Initializes a new instance of the StrategySimulation class. Declaration public StrategySimulation(Strategy strategy, double initialCash = 100) Parameters Type Name Description Strategy strategy The trading strategy to simulate. double initialCash The initial cash balance for the simulation (default is 100.0). Properties | Edit this page View Source Cash Gets the current cash balance in dollars. Declaration public double Cash { get; } Property Value Type Description double | Edit this page View Source InitialCash Gets the initial cash balance at simulation start. Declaration public double InitialCash { get; } Property Value Type Description double | Edit this page View Source Position Gets the current position size (positive for long, negative for short). Declaration public int Position { get; } Property Value Type Description int | Edit this page View Source SimulatedBook Gets the simulated order book containing bid/ask levels. Declaration public SimulatedOrderbook SimulatedBook { get; } Property Value Type Description SimulatedOrderbook | Edit this page View Source SimulatedRestingOrders Gets the list of resting orders placed during simulation. Each tuple contains (action, side, type, count, price, expiration). Declaration public List<(string action, string side, string type, int count, int price, DateTime? expiration)> SimulatedRestingOrders { get; } Property Value Type Description List<(string action, string side, string type, int count, int price, DateTime? expiration)> | Edit this page View Source Strategy Gets the trading strategy being simulated. Declaration public Strategy Strategy { get; } Property Value Type Description Strategy Methods | Edit this page View Source GetFinalEquity(MarketSnapshot) Declaration public double GetFinalEquity(MarketSnapshot lastSnapshot) Parameters Type Name Description MarketSnapshot lastSnapshot Returns Type Description double | Edit this page View Source ProcessSnapshot(MarketSnapshot, MarketSnapshot?) Processes a market snapshot by applying deltas, updating the order book, and executing trading decisions. This is the main entry point for advancing the simulation state with new market data. Declaration public void ProcessSnapshot(MarketSnapshot snapshot, MarketSnapshot? prevSnapshot) Parameters Type Name Description MarketSnapshot snapshot The current market snapshot to process, containing order book, position, and market data. MarketSnapshot prevSnapshot The previous market snapshot for delta calculation (optional). If provided, enables efficient delta-based updates. Remarks This method handles the core simulation loop with the following steps: Compute and apply order book deltas if previous snapshot exists (for efficiency) Simulate fills from those deltas on resting orders Initialize order book from snapshot if this is the first snapshot Create effective snapshot with simulated state overlay Get trading decision from strategy Apply the decision to update position, cash, and order book As a developer, this method orchestrates the entire simulation step, ensuring proper sequencing of market updates, strategy evaluation, and state changes. The effective snapshot approach allows strategies to see the current simulated state while maintaining separation between real market data and simulation artifacts.

obj/api/TradingStrategies.Trading.Overse.title: Namespace TradingStrategies.Trading.Overseer | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
obj/api/TradingStrategies.Trading.Overse.summary: Namespace TradingStrategies.Trading.Overseer Classes EquityCalculator Provides methods for calculating the total equity value of a trading simulation path. This class is responsible for determining the current market value of positions held in a simulated trading environment, taking into account both cash holdings and the unrealized value of open positions based on current order book data. MarketTypeService Service responsible for determining and caching market types for trading snapshots. This class acts as a facade over the MarketTypeHelper, providing caching functionality to avoid redundant market type calculations for the same market snapshot. PatternDetectionService Service responsible for detecting candlestick patterns from market snapshots in the Kalshi trading bot system. This service acts as a bridge between market data and pattern recognition algorithms, enabling trading strategies to incorporate technical analysis based on historical price movements. The service processes market snapshots containing recent candlestick data, converts them to a format suitable for pattern analysis, and applies comprehensive pattern detection algorithms to identify various technical analysis formations including single-candle, multi-candle, and complex patterns. Key responsibilities: Convert market snapshot candlestick data to pattern analysis format Execute pattern detection algorithms across multiple timeframes and pattern types Filter and return the most relevant patterns for trading decision making Handle edge cases and provide graceful fallbacks for data processing errors This service is used by the SimulationEngine during backtesting and strategy evaluation to enrich market analysis with technical pattern recognition capabilities. ReportGenerator Provides static methods for generating comprehensive performance reports from trading simulation event logs. This class processes collections of SimulationEventLog objects to produce detailed CSV-formatted reports that analyze trading performance, market statistics, strategy effectiveness, and simulation outcomes. It serves as the core reporting engine for the trading overseer system, enabling detailed analysis of backtesting results and strategy evaluation through structured data exports. ReportGenerator.EventGroup Represents a grouped collection of related event logs that share the same market type and action. This class aggregates multiple SimulationEventLog entries into summary statistics for analysis and reporting, providing averaged metrics and position changes over a time period. ReportGenerator.PathInfo Contains information about the strategies used in a specific trading path. This class provides a simple container for listing the strategy names that were active during a particular market condition or trading scenario. ReportGenerator.PathPerformance Represents the performance metrics for a complete trading path or simulation run. This class aggregates key performance indicators including profit/loss, equity changes, trade counts, and market state transitions for comprehensive analysis of trading outcomes. ReportGenerator.SimulationEventLog Represents a single event log entry from a trading simulation, capturing the complete state of the trading environment at a specific point in time. This data structure is used to record and analyze the progression of trading activities, market conditions, and strategy decisions throughout a simulation run. SimulationEngine Core engine for executing trading strategy simulations against historical market data. This class orchestrates the complete simulation lifecycle, processing market snapshots sequentially, applying trading strategies, simulating order execution and fills, managing position and cash flow, and generating detailed event logs for backtesting and performance analysis. SimulationPath Represents the complete state of a single trading simulation path in the Kalshi trading bot system. This class encapsulates all the essential data needed to track the evolution of a trading strategy simulation over time, including position, cash balance, risk metrics, and order book state. StrategySimulation Simulates trading strategies against historical market snapshots, managing position, cash, and order book state. This class processes market data sequentially, applies trading decisions from strategies, and tracks performance metrics. It maintains a simulated order book and resting orders to accurately model market interactions during backtesting. As a developer, this class is the core execution engine for strategy evaluation, handling the complex interplay between market data, strategy decisions, order book dynamics, and position management.

obj/api/TradingStrategies.html.summary: Namespace TradingStrategies Classes ActionDecision Represents the output of a trading strategy's decision-making process in the Kalshi trading bot system. This class encapsulates the recommended trading action, including the action type, associated parameters for order execution, and explanatory information. It serves as the communication mechanism between trading strategies and the simulation or execution engine, providing all necessary details for processing the recommended trade action.
